question_text: A metal wire is stretched beyond its elastic limit and does not return to its original length when the force is removed. Which statement best explains why this happens?

options.label: A
options.text: The force applied was too small to stretch the wire permanently.

options.label: B
options.text: The metal atoms have been rearranged and the bonds between them have changed position, so the wire cannot snap back to shape.

options.label: C
options.text: Hooke's law no longer applies because the wire is now too long.

options.label: D
options.text: The wire has lost all its elasticity and behaves like a plastic material.

explanation: When a material is stretched beyond its elastic limit, the atomic structure is permanently altered and the material cannot return to its original shape. This is called plastic deformation. Option B correctly identifies that atoms have shifted position permanently, unlike elastic deformation where they return to their original arrangement.
